items.question: What is the AI Storyboard Builder workflow?
items.answer: AI Storyboard Builder turns plain text scripts, scene descriptions, or beats into visual storyboard frames. It helps production teams quickly align on framing, pacing, and composition before committing time and budget to full storyboarding.

items.question: What kind of input works best for storyboard generation?
items.answer: Clear scene breakdowns work best: short descriptions of action, location, characters, and any important camera notes. You can paste script excerpts or bullet-pointed beats and then add notes about tone or visual style.

items.question: Does this replace a human storyboard artist?
items.answer: No. The workflow is ideal for fast previs, internal alignment, and early client decks—not a full replacement for professional storyboard artists. Many teams use AI boards as a starting point and then refine or redraw them later.

items.question: What kind of output can I expect from Storyboard Builder?
items.answer: The workflow generates sequences of storyboard-style frames with consistent characters, environments, and indicative camera angles. You can review them in OpenCreator, then export them as images or PDFs for collaboration.

items.question: Who is Storyboard Builder designed for?
items.answer: It is built for film and TV production teams, animation and game studios, agencies and commercial directors, and independent creators who need fast, clear visual plans from scripts or ideas.

items.question: How is this different from other storyboard-related templates?
items.answer: Storyboard Builder focuses on going from text to boards. Other templates like Sketches to Film or Script to Film are better suited for turning existing boards or scripts into moving sequences and animatics.

items.question: Can I annotate and reorder frames after generation?
items.answer: Yes. You can rearrange panels, add notes, and export organized boards for production, pitch decks, or client reviews. The AI handles initial generation; you stay in control of the final sequence.

items.question: How do credits work when using AI Storyboard Builder?
items.answer: Each generation consumes credits based on the underlying image and workflow steps. You can start with shorter scenes or key sequences to test the style before running longer or more complex scripts.
